access.user: Can query bus information and save preferences.
access.agent: Can read bus timetable capabilities and manage own bus preferences.

rules.public-no-signin: Bus is public campus life information; sign-in should not be a prerequisite.
rules.weekday-by-shanghai-time: The dashboard bus page determines weekday/weekend by Shanghai time by default and allows manual switching.
rules.shanghai-time-interpretation: When calculating 'how long until departure', whether a trip has departed, and route ordering, the dashboard client interprets HH:mm times in the timetable uniformly as Shanghai Time, not changing with the browser's timezone.
rules.core-filters-only: The dashboard bus page provides only core filters for departure stop, arrival stop, direction reversal, and 'show already-departed trips'; departure and arrival stops use a directly clickable vertical stop column to avoid secondary dropdowns, maintaining clear departure/arrival hierarchy.
rules.visual-priority: The dashboard visual structure should prioritize 'quick scanning of the next trip and route differences'; selected stop, direction, next departure time, and route grouping must be more prominent than decorative cards.
rules.public-home-next-departure-first: The anonymous home bus view leads with the next boardable departure and up to two following departures for the selected direction; it reuses a valid recent local route when no signed-in preference is available, while route controls and the complete grouped timetable remain accessible through progressive disclosure.
rules.sort-by-next-boardable: Routes are currently sorted by 'the next boardable time from the selected departure stop'; if a stop has no exact time in the original timetable, the average of adjacent known stop times in the same trip is used as an estimate.
rules.merged-table-grouped-by-route: The signed-in dashboard and the anonymous home's expanded timetable use a single merged large table to display all applicable routes, grouped into table sections by route; each section shows the visible trips for that route.
rules.raw-data-returned: /api/bus and query_bus_timetable return raw route/stop/trip data within the selected timetable version; the preferences field is null for anonymous callers and returns the authenticated caller's saved preferences; the server does not pre-apply recommendations, filtering, or sorting.
rules.version-topology-self-contained: When versionKey selects a historical timetable version, routes, stops, campuses, and trips are derived from that same imported schedule version rather than the latest global route topology.
rules.current-version-only: list_bus_routes only lists routes actually present in the current active timetable version; it does not expose historical route IDs outside the current version.
rules.dashboard-vs-map: The dashboard prioritizes answering 'how to get the next trip'; the map page handles spatial understanding.
rules.public-version-metadata-omitted: Public bus dashboard pages omit timetable version metadata rather than showing an unlabeled or unlocalized imported value; the raw title remains available to admin, REST, and MCP consumers.
rules.responsive-route-surfaces: Bus planner and transit-map pages keep document content within narrow viewports, provide at least 44 CSS pixels of height for primary route actions, wrap stop sequences only between complete arrow-and-stop tokens, and keep campus labels legible over route strokes.
rules.task-oriented-tool-hierarchy: Use query_bus_timetable for raw/full datasets, list_bus_routes for unfiltered discovery, search_bus_routes for filtered discovery, get_bus_route_timetable for one route, get_next_buses for immediate departure answers, and get_my_bus_preferences/save_my_bus_preferences for personal defaults.
rules.canonical-compact-mode: query_bus_timetable default mode returns compact counts, route/campus summaries, and relevant departures rather than the full trip dataset; the deprecated summary input is an alias for this same shape.

routes.returns: { locale, fetchedAt, version, availableVersions, campuses, routes, trips, preferences, notice }

notes: Unknown non-null preferred origin or destination campus IDs return 400 with { error } and do not write preferences.

queries.name: busRoutes
notes: The GraphQL page argument bounds the route catalog to pageSize 1-100.

queries.name: busTimetable
notes: The input selects one route and bounds weekday and weekend trip slots to pageSize 1-100.
The optional now argument is a DateTime and must include an explicit Z or numeric timezone offset.

tools.name: query_bus_timetable
tools.returns: { locale, fetchedAt, version, availableVersions, campuses, routes, trips, preferences, notice }
notes: default mode returns a compact assistant-oriented snapshot with counts, summarized campuses/routes, and relevant nextDepartures instead of the full trip dataset; deprecated summary is an alias for default.
Request full mode for the raw campuses/routes/trips dataset.

tools.name: list_bus_routes
notes: Use this for full current-version route discovery before fetching one route timetable.

tools.name: get_bus_route_timetable
notes: Single-route raw timetable tool; use get_next_buses when the caller only needs the next few departures.

tools.name: get_my_bus_preferences
notes: Returns the authenticated caller's saved bus preferences or the default null-campus preference.

tools.name: save_my_bus_preferences
notes: preferredOriginCampusId and preferredDestinationCampusId may be null to reset saved campuses.
Unknown non-null preferred origin or destination campus IDs return a serialized validation result with success=false and do not write preferences.

routes.returns: { originCampus?, destinationCampus?, total: Int, routes: BusRouteSearchResult[] }
notes: Filtered route discovery helper for origin/destination clients.
Preserves concrete route variants rather than merging routes with the same campus pair.

tools.name: search_bus_routes
tools.returns: { originCampus?, destinationCampus?, total: Int, routes: BusRouteSearchResult[] }
notes: Filtered discovery helper for origin/destination prompts.
Use list_bus_routes when the caller needs the entire current route catalog.

routes.returns: { originCampus, destinationCampus, atTime, dayType, totalRoutes: Int, departures: BusDeparture[], nextAvailableDeparture?, message? }
notes: Requires originCampusId and destinationCampusId.
Supports atTime, dayType, includeDeparted, limit, versionKey, and locale query parameters.
REST and MCP share the same limit bounds: 1-50.
Departures include status and minutesUntilDeparture so clients can mark departed rows and the next upcoming trip.

tools.name: get_next_buses
tools.returns: { originCampus, destinationCampus, atTime, dayType, totalRoutes: Int, departures: BusDeparture[] }
notes: Task-oriented convenience tool for 'when is the next bus?' prompts.
Default parameters: dayType=auto, includeDeparted=false, limit=5; REST and MCP share limit bounds 1-50.
When a route exists but no remaining departures are available for the requested window, return guidance describing the situation and, when possible, the next available service time.
Default and the deprecated summary alias avoid repeating the same originCampus/destinationCampus object inside every departure item when they already appear at the top level.

fields: Active trips derived from timetable times
